mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns as capital flows into risk assets.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) following rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.5 percent, consumer confidence collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 9.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand recovery, which sustains corporate revenue growth and produces positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model with a Sharpe ratio above 0.5, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 60 percent of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates capacity utilization improvements and margin expansion, triggering short-term positive equity momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of periods following positive INDPRO momentum, or the mechanism shows zero predictive accuracy during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25 percent, quantitative easing expectations dominate pricing, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 percent, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and persistently high unemployment indicates labor hoarding and weak productivity, which suppresses corporate profitability and yields negative quarterly returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no statistical significance in predicting 60-day returns, or positive returns occur in over 60 percent of divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real GDP growth directly boost corporate earnings expectations, creating immediate positive price pressure on equity indices over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no correlation with GDPC1 changes, or the Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 95 percent of its pre-crisis peak during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and inflationary pressure, prompting risk-off positioning and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of periods when INDPRO exceeds 95, or the signal fails to predict direction in out-of-sample tests.
